[File: 'src/SableRewarder.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: issueSABLE()] Can an unprivileged attacker (any EOA or contract) under the precondition that issueSABLE() lacks a caller-restriction modifier (no _requireCallerIsSableStaking equivalent) call issueSABLE() directly, bypassing the stake/unstake flow, triggering increaseF_SABLE(_issuance) on SableStakingV2 and a sableToken.transfer to SableStakingV2 without any LP stake action, violating the invariant that F_SABLE must only increase when called through the authorized SableStakingV2 stake/unstake path, causing scoped impact: attacker-controlled staker pre-positions a stake, then externally calls issueSABLE() repeatedly to inflate F_SABLE and drain SABLE from the rewarder into SableStakingV2 faster than the intended emission schedule, then unstakes to extract excess SABLE? Local proof idea: set rate=1e18, wait T seconds, call updateRewardPerSec(10e18), then call stake() which triggers issueSABLE(); assert issuance = 10e18 * T instead of 1e18 * T.
